observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:


     1. Direct the OIC -  Municipal  Accountant  to  fast  track  the  full
        validation of the identified reconciling items for  preparation  of
        necessary adjustments and journal entries to account  the  same  in
        the books.


     2. Direct the OIC – Municipal Accountant to  prepare  and  submit  the
        BRSs for all accounts at the prescribed period  to  facilitate  the
        conduct of timely review on the same.

We recommended the following courses of action:


1. Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash  advances  thru
   the issuance of demand letters to officials and employees  concerned  and
   strictly comply with  the  provisions  of  COA  Circular  97-002  in  the
   granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

2. Conduct regular and periodic verification and analysis of  the  existence
   of receivables and unliquidated cash advances to determine the extent  of
   collectability.

3. Request for the write-off of dormant accounts and  submit  the  certified
   relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions.

4.  Stop  the  practice  of  granting  cash  advance   to   personnel   with
   unliquidated balance.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal  Accounting  Office  and
General Service Office to reconcile their records  of  the  Property,  Plant
and Equipment accounts and make the necessary adjustments in  the  books  of
accounts  if  warranted.  Further,  secure  the  copies  of  the   documents
pertaining to the Awuyon Community Hospital Building from the Department  of
Health and record the same as asset of  the  Municipality  and  provide  the
correct amount of deprecation in the books of accounts.

We recommended the following courses of action:


     1. Conduct a thorough verification  on  the  nature/purpose  of  these
        funds to determine the status  of  the  projects  covered  by  such
        funds;


     2. Determine if the remaining amounts pertain to unutilized portion of
        the  funds  received  after  project  completion  and   cause   the
        remittance/return of such to the source agencies; and


     3.  For  ongoing  projects  with  remaining  balances  in  the  books,
        coordinate with the source agencies to reconcile the balances  with
        their records and prepare the necessary correcting entries (if any)
        to arrive at the accurate balances of the Due to NGAs account as at
        reporting date.

We recommended that management direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:


1. Determine the validity of the said  accounts  and  communicate  with  the
   payees for the immediate payment/settlement of the accounts;
2. Revert the balances of these long outstanding payables  pursuant  to  the
   provisions of PD 1445 so that the corresponding  fund  may  be  used  for
   other projects of the municipality;


3. Make the necessary adjusting entries to  properly  classify  the  payable
   accounts; and

4.  Refrain  from  accruing  expenses  or  recording  assets  without  valid
   documentation and without proper verification of the actual  delivery  of
   goods or incurrence of expense.

We recommended the following to Management:


1. Require the BAC Secretariat and the Municipal Accountant  to  submit  the
   complete documentary  requirements  on  the  above-mentioned  procurement
   transactions;

2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the lacking DVs;

3. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the  complete  supporting
   documents are attached to the DVs before submission to COA; and

4. Direct the BAC Secretariat to comply with the  posting  requirements  for
   transactions using Negotiated Procurement-Emergency Case  as  a  mode  of
   procurement.

We recommended that Management fully utilize the  BGCM  fund  for  COVID-19
related programs and activities. Otherwise, the balance shall  be  reverted
to the National Treasury such that the same may be made available for other
programs of the National Government.

We recommended the following courses of action:

   1. Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding  fund  transfers
      thru the issuance of letters of demand to the concerned barangays and
      strictly comply with the aforementioned provisions in  the  granting,
      utilization and liquidation of fund transfers;

   2. Refrain from granting fund  transfers  to  any  barangay  unless  the
      previously transferred funds are settled or proper accounting thereof
      is made;

   3. For subsequent fund transfers, ensure that relevant  and  appropriate
      provisions be included in  the  MOA  to  ensure  timely  liquidation,
      utilization and return of unexpended amounts.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the OIC – Municipal  Accountant
to:

   1. Maintain a special account in the General Fund for the 20% Development
      Fund; and


   2. Prepare the Quarterly and Annual Fund Receipt and  Utilization  Report
      for Other Special Accounts, and submit the FRUR to the Office  of  the
      Auditor on or before the 15th day of the month after each quarter.

We recommended  that  Management  adhere  strictly  to  existing  guidelines
pertaining to the preparation of LDRRMFIP and AIP before utilization of  the
LDRRMF.  Further,  submit  a  written  explanation  of  the   aforementioned
deficiency  for  us  to  evaluate  whether  to  suspend  or   disallow   the
transactions in the audit.

We recommended to Management the following:

1. Require the drivers/end-users to completely  and  properly  fill  in  the
   required information in the Driver’s Trip  Tickets  and  to  prepare  the
   Monthly Report of Official Travels;

2. Designate personnel to be in charge of the monitoring of the issuance  of
   RIVs and preparation of the  Daily  Gas  Issue  Record/Log  Book  or  its
   equivalent, and Fuel Consumption Report based on the format prescribed in
   the manual; and

3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to review the completeness  and  accuracy
   of the  supporting  documents  for  fuel  consumption/utilization  before
   payment and to submit the lacking DVs and supporting  documents  for  the
   above-mentioned fuel payments

We recommended to Management the following:

   1. Stop the grant of overtime  pay  to  Municipal  Government  Department
      Heads I as they are not authorized to such claim;


   2. Require the Municipal Accountant to  submit  immediately  the  lacking
      disbursement vouchers/ payrolls and  ensure  that  employees/personnel
      claiming overtime pay are included in the list of personnel authorized
      by the Municipal Mayor to render overtime services;


   3. Provide appropriate funds for the payment  of  overtime  pay  of  non-
      plantilla personnel; and

   4. Submit a justification for the above-mentioned deficiencies.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere  to  the  guidelines  on  the
grant  and  liquidation  of  cash  advances  for  CF  specifically  on   the
submission of the disbursement vouchers  and  supporting  documents  to  the
Office of the Auditor within seven (7) days after the release of the  check.
Further, stop the  practice  of  granting  cash  advance  to  the  SDO  with
unliquidated cash advances.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  be  required  to   submit
immediately  the  lacking  disbursement  vouchers  complete  with   relevant
supporting documents  and  to  keenly  observe  the  deadline  set  for  the
submission of trial balances and other financial reports as  required  under
Section  122  of  PD  1445  and  other  relevant  COA  issuances.   Further,
Management shall review its policies and procedures  on  the  processing  of
transactions such that causes of bottleneck and delays may be  corrected  to
streamline the procurement and disbursement process of the agency.

We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:


1. Provide an action plan to address the prevailing issue/problem  including
   a projected timeline for implementation;

2. Fast track and prioritize the conduct of meetings and consultations  with
   stakeholders,  to  address  the  issue  of  relocation  of  the  informal
   settlers, and to finalize the proposed ordinance for  the  operations  of
   the Eco Park;

3. Allocate funds for the procurement of supplies,  provision  of  manpower,
   repairs, and other requirements for its full operation.

We recommended the Management to:

a) Provide an action plan to address the prevailing issue/problem  including
   a projected timeline of implementation;

b) Facilitate the immediate transfer of  the  slaughterhouse  operations  to
   its new facility; and

c) Allocate funds for the repairs on the structure.

We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:


1. Fast track the completion of  the  Commercial  Building  to  prevent  the
   completed portion of the project from being dilapidated or damaged. Anent
   thereto, require the MEO to conduct an inspection on the extent of  works
   completed and to prepare  and  submit  a  report  thereon  as  basis  for
   recommendation on whether a  variation  order  is  required.  Review  the
   requirements of the suspension order to determine whether the same  shall
   be lifted and the supplier may resume completion of the work, or if  non-
   compliance of the supplier warrants the termination of the  work  covered
   by the suspension order.


2. Require  the  MPDO  and  MEO  to  exercise  diligently  their  monitoring
   function such that issues and concerns that  cause  a  delay  in  project
   implementation may be immediately identified and addressed.

3. Submit a written justification for the above-mentioned deficiencies.

We  recommended  that  management  fast-track  the  implementation  of
developmental projects as embodied in the AIP in accordance  with  the
prescribed schedule of  implementation  to  accelerate  socio-economic
growth and environmental development in the municipality.  We  further
recommend to the MPDO and MEO to exercise diligently their  monitoring
function such that issues and concerns that would cause a delay in the
project implementation could be immediately identified and addressed.

Recommendations:


      We recommended the following courses of action:


a. Enforce the  immediate  liquidation  of  the  outstanding  cash  advances
   through the issuance of letters of  demand  to  officials  and  employees
   concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular  97-002
   in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

b. Conduct regular and periodic verification and analysis of  the  existence
   of receivables and unliquidated cash advances to determine the extent  of
   collectability.

c. Request for the write-off of dormant accounts and  submit  the  certified
   relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions.

Recommendation:


      We recommended  that  management  require  the  SDOs  to  prepare  and
maintain an updated cashbook for  their  cash  advances  to  facilitate  the
verification, monitoring and reconciliation of the same against the  ledgers
kept by the Municipal Accounting Office.

Recommendation:


      We recommended that  management  issue  immediately  an  office  order
designating the aforesaid four (4) AOs as special disbursing officers.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that management enforce the  immediate  liquidation  of
these fund transfers or resort to legal remedies if warranted,  pursuant  to
Section 9 of COA Circular 2007-001.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accounting  Office
and General Service Office to  reconcile  their  records  of  the  Property,
Plant and Equipment accounts.

Recommendation:

      We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct   the   Municipal
Accountant to provide  depreciation  expenses  for  all  depreciable  assets
which must be recorded regularly, to present fairly the PPE accounts in  the
financial statements as at end of each reporting period.  Further,  to  make
the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts  to  recognize  the
unrecorded depreciation expenses for CY 2019.

Recommendation:

      We recommended  that  adjusting  entries  be  made  by  the  Municipal
Accountant in the General Fund books to  recognize  the  payments  made  for
labor as PPE. Further, compute and record the related  depreciation  expense
to reflect the correct PPE book balance at year-end.

Recommendation:

      We recommended  that  adjusting  entries  be  made  by  the  Municipal
Accounting Office in the General Fund books to recognize  the  MRF  and  the
SWM Equipment as PPE. Further, compute and record the  related  depreciation
expense to reflect the correct PPE book balance at year-end.

Recommendation:

      We recommended  that  adjusting  entries  be  made  by  the  Municipal
Accounting Office in the General Fund  books  to  reflect  the  correct  PPE
account balance as at year-end.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the following courses of action to management:


     a) Require the Municipal Accountant to record the biological assets in
        the books of accounts to reflect the correct balance of the account
        in the financial  statements.  The  accounting  of  the  biological
        assets  and   changes   thereof   brought   about   by   biological
        transformations must be in accordance with PPSAS 27.


     b) Require the Municipal Agriculturist Office  to  maintain  livestock
        ledger  cards  and  ensure  that  subsequent  births,  deaths,  and
        dispositions of the  animals  will  be  recorded.  Also,  regularly
        submit an inventory report to the Municipal Accounting  Office  for
        recording.

Recommendations:

      We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:


     a. Submit a written explanation on the  noted  erroneous  transfer  of
        unexpended appropriations of the current year  LDRRMF  for  CO  and
        continuing appropriations to the Trust Fund books.


     b. Closely  monitor  the  fund  utilization  of  the  said  unexpended
        balances to ensure the full implementation of the PPAs for which it
        was originally allotted.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that management discontinue the use of 20% DF  for the
payment of administrative expenses to preclude the  issuance  of  suspension
or disallowance in the audit.

Recommendations:

      We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:


   a) Enforce timely reporting  of  disbursements  and  liquidation  to  the
      source  agencies  of  the  fund  transfers   received   to   ascertain
      reconciliation of records between agencies.


   b)  Submit  the  necessary  reports  to  the  respective  NGAs  for   the
      reconciliation of the discrepancies noted, and furnish a copy  to  the
      Office of the Auditor for reference and validation.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the following courses of action:


     1. Require the department heads/designated supervisors to prepare  the
        statement of duties and responsibilities  as  the  basis  for  work
        assignments and to ensure that actual accomplishments are relevant
        to the requirements of the office;


     2. Require job orders to prepare  and  submit  accomplishment  reports
        that are specific in terms of actual  duties  performed  such  that
        proper evaluation may be made on the necessity of hiring/re-hiring.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that management facilitate the institutionalization  of
a functional GAD Database  which  shall  aid  in  gender  analysis  for  the
identification of gender issues and the cause/causes,  to  be  addressed  by
the agency.

Recommendations:

      We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:


     a. Facilitate the immediate implementation of the strategies laid down
        in the ESWM Plan and the  acquisition  of  necessary  equipment  by
        appropriating sufficient funds. Also, the Management is reminded to
        prioritize the construction of the sanitary  landfill  as  a  final
        disposal site.


     b. Give emphasis during IEC on the critical role of barangays  in  the
        full implementation of the ESWM  Plan  by  offering  incentives  or
        imposing sanctions pursuant to Section 45 and 50 of RA 9003.

Recommendations:

      We  recommended  that  management  fast-track  the  implementation  of
developmental projects as embodied  in  the  AIP  following  the  prescribed
schedule  of  implementation  to  accelerate   socio-economic   growth   and
environmental development in the municipality. We further recommend  to  the
MPDO and MEO to exercise diligently  their  monitoring  function  such  that
issues and concerns that would cause a delay in the  project  implementation
could be immediately identified and addressed.

Recommendation:

      We  recommended  that  MDRRMO  judiciously  prepare  the  LDRRMF  Plan
incorporating  time  schedules,  availability   of   resources   and   other
contingencies to avoid delay in the implementation  of  PPAs.  Further,  the
MDRRMO is enjoined to improve the utilization rate of  LDRRMF  and  expedite
the implementation of identified PPAs, to strengthen the  agency’s  capacity
in mitigating and preparing for,  responding  to  and  recovering  from  the
impact of disasters.

Recommendations:

      We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:


   a) Judiciously plan the implementation of projects  funded  by  NGAs  and
      ensure that it  is  in  accordance  with  the  prescribed  guidelines.
      Further, proper coordination should be made among the stakeholders  to
      assure that issues and conflicts during the project implementation are
      timely addressed.


   b) Provide an action plan to address the prevailing issue/problem on  the
      identified nonoperational projects.
Adopt a proper and effective inventory and supplies management system and maintain the required records and reports as required in COA Circular 92-386 and the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Funds for the construction of warehouse was included in CY 2019 budget.
observations.reason: The management is still unable to adopt a proper and effective inventory and supplies management system.

observations.recommendation: Refrain from using LDRRMF for programs, projects and activities which are not directly related with disaster preparedness, mitigation, and response and rescue operations and strictly adhere to the provisions of existing rules and regulations in the proper utilization of the fund.
observations.reason: The management still practice the charging of PPAs not directly related to disaster risk reduction and management activities. Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: Ensure that charges to the fund are aligned with its purpose and fast track the implementation of PPAs during the year such that the objectives for the provision of said funds may be met.
observations.reason: The OIC Budget Officer was allegedly not aware that accomplishments of the Jos were not aligned with the purpose of the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to submit Contracts/POs and its supporting documents on time as prescribed under Sections 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 and ensure that the same are properly accomplished indicating such details as date of delivery, delivery term, payment term and date signed by the supplier. Priority should be given to the unsubmitted Contracts/POs is highly needed.
observations.reason: The submission of POs and contracts was still not within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: Direct the LDRRMO to ensure that LDRRMF shall be exclusively utilized pursuant to the provisions of RA 10121 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.
observations.reason: The management still practice the charging of PPAs not directly related to disaster risk reduction and management activities due to insufficiency of funds to support other operating expenses.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the provisions of Section 48 of NGAS for LGUs and COA Circular 2007-002 regarding the imprest fund system in maintaining petty cash fund and to follow the illustrative entries indicated above in recording petty cash transactions. Direct the Petty Cash Custodians/Accountable Officers that disbursement of petty cash fund shall be made through petty cash vouchers and ensure proper and complete documentation thereof.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: Petty cash fund of the municipality is still not in accordance with the imprest fund system.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the granting of cash advances. Personnel with outstanding cash advances should not be granted additional cash advances. Strictly enforce settlement of all unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2016, particularly those pertaining to previous years.
observations.reason: Multiple cash advances were still granted to the same AO since there are only a few Disbursing Officers. Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2018.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Accounting Office and Government Service Office to reconcile the Property, Plant and Equipment Accounts.
observations.reason: The GSO and the Accounting Office have yet to complete the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Accounting Office and Government Service Office to reconcile the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts.
observations.reason: The GSO and the Accounting Office have yet to complete the reconciliation. Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2018.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to strictly comply with the provisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular such that only allowable expenses are charged to the fund.
observations.reason: Inappropriate charges to the fund were still made due to insufficiency of funds to support other operating expenses.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Accounting Office to record purchases of supplies and materials as Inventory. Direct the designated Property and Supply Officer to strictly monitor the issuances of supplies and materials to end users. Requisition and Issue Slip shall be prepared upon issuance of supplies. The Property and Supply Officer designate shall consolidate the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued and prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and forward to Accounting Unit.
observations.managementAction: Several purchases of supplies and materials were directly charged to expenses
observations.reason: The management is still unable to adopt a proper and effective inventory and supplies management system.

observations.recommendation: Advice the Municipal Accountant to trace back to the time when the subject reconciling items were recognized so that appropriate adjustment to correct the deficiency should be made.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: Relevant documents are yet to be located.

observations.recommendation: Conduct an investigation to determine who were responsible in the incurrence of questionable reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements which were eventually taken up as prior years’ adjustments. The officials responsible for such loss of funds should be held liable for the restitution thereof.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: Relevant documents are yet to be located.

observations.recommendation: Submit the required and missing documents to prove actual incurrence of obligation for catering services expenses as well as in procurement of other food items. Likewise, submit explanations and justifications as to: a. Necessity and significance of the subject activities/functions to contribute to the attainment of objectives of the related programs for social and economic development to promote the general welfare of the people as required under Section 16 of R.A. No. 7160. b. How and why conferences, meetings or activities provided with meals and snacks are related or incidental to efficient and effective provision of the basic services and facilities required under Section 17 of R.A. No. 7160. c. Why the lists of participants, if available, contained names of persons, whose participation were of no relevance to the activity. The recorded number of persons who participated in the activities to incur such expenses in big amounts for catering services/ meals and snacks should likewise be justified.
observations.managementAction: Per AAR CY 2011, disbursements for catering services were duly supported by the appropriate and required documents, however, submission of the required documents and explanation/justification were not yet complied with by management.
observations.reason: Pertinent documents and records can no longer be found.

observations.recommendation: Require the office in charge in determining the recipients to be granted educational assistance to submit to the Office of the Auditor pertinent documents of the beneficiaries. Likewise, it is emphasized that strict selection process should be undertaken by the LGU.
observations.reason: Pertinent documents and records can no longer be found.

observations.recommendation: Strictly enforce the settlement of disallowance by all persons liable that will be issued on the transactions involving payment of unauthorized cost of living allowance in total amount of P4,206,395.00.
observations.reason: The copy of the ND requiring the agency to refund the COLA cannot be located. The same is also not reported in the SASDC.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the immediate submission of  the  necessary  supporting
documents  that  would  support  adjustments  made  on  the  bank  and  book
reconciling items.

We recommended the following courses of action:


     a. Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash  advances
        thru the issuance of letters of demand to officials  and  employees
        concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of  COA  Circular
        97-002  in  the  granting,  utilization  and  liquidation  of  cash
        advances.
     b. Conduct regular and  periodic  verification  and  analysis  of  the
        existence  of  receivables  and  unliquidated  cash   advances   to
        determine extent of collectability.


     c. Request for the  write-off  of  dormant  accounts  and  submit  the
        certified  relevant  documents  validating  the  existence  of  the
        conditions.

We recommended that management direct Municipal Accounting Office  and
General Service Office  to  reconcile  the  Property,  Plant  and  Equipment
accounts and to effect the necessary  adjusting  entries  in  the  books  of
accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately  transfer  the
fund balances received from NGAs to the Trust Fund bank  account  and  OIC-
Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that  OIC-Municipal  Accountant  effect  the  necessary
adjusting journal entries to reflect the  correct  account  balances  as  at
year-end.
We recommended that proper recording of transactions should be strictly observed to prevent against inaccurate information.
observations.managementAction: Proper recording of cash deposits was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer conduct periodic review of outstanding checks to determine which checks are about to become stale in order that necessary corrections will be made. The accountant should make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct cash in bank balance at year end.
observations.managementAction: The management regularly monitor its outstanding checks to determine which checks are about to become stale.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that adjusting entries be made by the Municipal Accountant to recognize the completed projects in the General Fund books and to record the construction in progress account under Trust Fund. Moreover, compute and record the related depreciation expense to reflect the correct PPE book balance at year end. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant closely monitor and review the recording of transactions in the books.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting journal entries for the recognition of assets were made in 1st quarter of CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: Direct the MEO and MPDO to conduct regular monitoring on the status of implementation of projects funded by the municipality and require the barangays to submit the terminal report as prescribed in the MOA.
observations.managementAction: The MEO already conducted regular monitoring on the implementati on in the barangays.
observations.reason: The submission of terminal reports by the barangays was not given preferential attention.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prioritize the hiring of three (3) technical staff at the LDRRMO to assist the existing LDRRM Officer III in carrying out disaster risk reduction and management functions.
observations.managementAction: One (1) technical staff for LDRMO was hired in CY 2018. The other two (2) required positions were already created and budgeted. Hiring process thereof will commence in first quarter of CY 2019.
observations.reason: The other two (2) required technical LDRMO staff were still not hired as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMO should prepare monthly the Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to be certified by the Municipal Accountant and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor through the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) and Local Development Council (LDC) on or before 15th day of the following month.
observations.managementAction: The required LDRRM reports were regularly submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: Fast track the implementation of PPAs during the year in order that the objectives for the provision of said funds will be met.
observations.reason: Implementation of PPAs related to solid waste management as indicated in the plan is still on-going

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, upon receipt of the assessment roll from the Municipal Assessor of the assessment roll, shall compute the amount due for RPT and SET receivables and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for proper accrual of amount due to reflect the correct RPT/SET receivable balance at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted the RPT/SET computation to the MACCO on time and the same was properly recorded in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that funds are utilized in accordance with its intended purpose and fast track the implementation of the PPAs during the year.
observations.managementAction: All PPAs related to LCPC were implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant follow the required amount of employer premium contribution as authorized under R.A. 9679.
observations.managementAction: The government's share for HDMF premiums was already reduced to P100.00 in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: Submit a written justification for the failure to submit for post audit the DVs and supporting documents on cash advances granted for CF. Strictly adhere to the guidelines on the grant and liquidation of cash advances for CF.
observations.managementAction: Written justification was already submitted to the Office of the Auditor. The proper process for the grant and liquidation of cash advances for CF was followed in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: Ensure that cash advances are supported with the required documentation; Observe strictly the proper recording of the grant and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Complete documentation for the grant and liquidation of cash advances for payroll was observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of action: Designate another permanently appointed official/employee to perform disbursing functions and ensure that the disbursing officer is adequately bonded. Adhere strictly to the laws, rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as required under Section 339 of R.A. 7160 and Section 4.1.4 of COA Circular 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Special Disbursing Officers were designated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of action: Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances thru the issuance of letters of demand to officials and employees concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Conduct regular and periodic verification and analysis of the existence of receivables and unliquidated cash advances to determine extent of collectability. Request for the write-off of dormant accounts and submit the certified relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were already sent by the Municipal Accountant to the concerned employees and officials.
observations.reason: Demand Letters were not received by AOs/ personnel whose whereabouts cannot be determined. Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: Direct the Accounting and General Service Offices to reconcile the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation expense of the depreciable assets in order to allocate its cost over their useful life and for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Development Planning Officer to hasten the implementation of the said above projects.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Planning Development Council or the Municipal Project Engineer to expedite the implementation of developmental projects embodied in the Municipal Annual Investment Plan within its time frame to accelerate socio-economic growth and environmental development in the barangay and to exercise diligently their monitoring function such that issues and concerns that cause delay in project implementation may be immediately identified and addressed.
observations.managementAction: Other projects are implemented within the year
observations.reason: There are still two (2) projects that are currently on-going as to implementation and other two (2) that are still not implemented.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF monthly and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor through the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) and Local Development Council (LDC).
observations.managementAction: The required reports were regularly submitted to the Office of the Auditor
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2017 and 2018

observations.recommendation: Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that the provisions of R.A.9184 pertaining to procurement done through alternative modes be strictly followed. Also, reimbursements should be limited to cases where such supplies/materials or services cannot be provided or rendered unless immediate payment is made. Lastly, we advise to issue an internal memorandum defining the limits on the amount that may be reimbursed including the officers and employees that may be authorized to do so.
observations.managementAction: Reimbursements were strictly limited to emergency cases.
observations.reason: Minimal amount of reimbursements was still made during the year.

observations.recommendation: Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to compute depreciation expense on its depreciable assets in order to allocate its cost over their useful life and for fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: All PPE accounts were already provided with depreciation in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the aforementioned paid disbursement vouchers to the Commission on Audit so that timely audit of the transactions of the municipality can be performed.
observations.managementAction: All DVs were submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the granting of cash advances. Personnel with outstanding cash advances should not be granted additional cash advances. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to personnel with unliquidated cash advances with emphasis to have those balances that remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2011 immediately settled.
observations.reason: Although Demand Letters were indeed issued, management has yet to determine the whereabouts of some AOs/Personnel.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Cash in Bank account per ledger and per cashbook monthly or as often as necessary.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to prepare the Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF monthly duly certified by the Local Accountant and submit same to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Bids and Awards Committee to see to it that the provisions of R.A.9184 pertaining to procurement done through Shopping should be strictly followed. Reimbursements should be limited to cases where such supplies/materials or services cannot be provided or rendered unless immediate payment is made. It is advised that an internal memorandum be issued to define the limits on the amount that may be reimbursed including the officers and employees that may be authorized to do so.
observations.managementAction: Reimbursements were strictly limited to emergency cases. A memo was not issued but management strictly monitors the procurements made thru reimbursements.

observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR 2017

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the provisions of Section 48 of NGAS for LGUs and COA Circular 2007-002 regarding the imprest fund system in maintaining petty cash fund and to follow the illustrative entries indicated above in recording petty cash transactions. Direct the Petty Cash Custodians/Accountable Officers that disbursement of petty cash fund shall be made through petty cash vouchers and ensure proper and complete documentation thereof.
observations.managementAction: No Action taken
observations.reason: Petty cash fund of the municipality is still not in accordance with the imprest fund system.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the granting of cash advances. Personnel with outstanding cash advances should not be granted additional cash advances unless previous ones are liquidated. Strictly enforce settlement of all unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2016, particularly those pertaining to previous years.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR 2017

observations.managementAction: Not all PPEs are reconciled to the two (2) independent records.

Personnel with outstanding cash advances should not be granted additional cash advances unless previous ones are liquidated. observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR 2017

observations.recommendation: Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to strictly comply with the provisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular such that only allowable expenses are charged to the fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR 2017

observations.recommendation: Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to prepare the Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF monthly duly certified by the Local Accountant and submit same to the Office of the Auditor.

Advice the Municipal Accountant to trace back to the time when the subject reconciling items were recognized so that appropriate adjustment to correct the deficiency should be made. Conduct an investigation to determine who were responsible in the incurrence of questionable reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements which were eventually taken up as prior years’ adjustments. The officials responsible for such loss of funds should be held liable for the restitution thereof.
observations.managementAction: No investigation conducted

observations.recommendation: Submit the required and missing documents to prove actual incurrence of obligation for catering services as well as in procurement of other food items. Likewise, submit explanations and justifications as to: a. Necessity and significance of the subject activities/functions to contribute to the attainment of objectives of the related programs for social and economic development to promote the general welfare of the people as required under Section 16 of R.A. No. 7160. b. How and why conferences, meetings or activities provided with meals and snacks are related or incidental to efficient and effective provision of the basic services and facilities required under Section 17 of R.A. No. 7160. c. Why the lists of participants, if available, contained names of persons, whose participation were of no relevance to the activity. The recorded number of persons who participated in the activities to incur such expenses in big amounts for catering services/meals and snacks should likewise be justified.
observations.managementAction: Per AAR CY 2011, disbursement s for catering services were duly supported by the appropriate and required documents, however, submission of the required documents and explanation/justification were not yet complied with by management.

observations.recommendation: Require the office in charge in determining the recipients to be granted educational assistance to submit to the Office of the Auditor pertinent documents of the beneficiaries. Likewise, it is emphasized that strict selection process should be undertaken by the LGU.

Strictly enforce the settlement of disallowance by all persons liable that will be issued on the transactions involving payment of unauthorized cost of living allowance in total amount of P4,206,395.00.

Recommendation:

      We  recommended  that  proper  recording  of  transactions  should  be
strictly observed to prevent against inaccurate information.

Recommendation:


      We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer conduct periodic review of
outstanding checks to determine which checks are about to  become  stale  in
order that necessary corrections will be made. The  accountant  should  make
the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct cash in bank  balance
at year end.

Recommendations:


      We recommended  that  adjusting  entries  be  made  by  the  Municipal
Accountant to recognize the completed projects in  the  General  Fund  books
and to record  the  construction  in  progress  account  under  Trust  Fund.
Moreover, compute and record the related  depreciation  expense  to  reflect
the correct PPE book balance at year end.


      We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant  closely  monitor
and review the recording of transactions in the books.

Recommendations:

      Adopt a proper and effective inventory and supplies management  system
and maintain the required records and reports as required  in  COA  Circular
92-386 and the NGAS Manual.

Recommendations:

      Direct the MEO and MPDO to conduct regular monitoring  on  the  status
of implementation of projects funded by the  municipality  and  require  the
barangays to submit the terminal report as prescribed in the MOA.

Recommendations:


      Refrain from using LDRRMF for programs, projects and activities  which
are  not  directly  related  with  disaster  preparedness,  mitigation,  and
response and rescue operations and strictly  adhere  to  the  provisions  of
existing rules and regulations in the proper utilization of the fund.

Recommendation:


      We recommended that management prioritize  the  hiring  of  three  (3)
technical staff at the LDRRM Office to assist  the  existing  LDRRM  Officer
III in carrying out disaster risk reduction and management functions.

Recommendation:

            We recommended  that  the  LDRRMO  should  prepare  monthly  the
Report of  Sources  and  Utilization  of  LDRRMF  to  be  certified  by  the
Municipal Accountant and submit the  same  to  the  Office  of  the  Auditor
through the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management  Council  (LDRRMC)  and
Local Development Council (LDC) on or  before  15th  day  of  the  following
month.

Recommendations:

      Fast track the implementation of PPAs during the year  in  order  that
the objectives for the provision of said funds will be met.

Recommendation:


      We recommended that the  Municipal  Treasurer,  upon  receipt  of  the
assessment roll from the Municipal Assessor of the  assessment  roll,  shall
compute the amount due for RPT and SET receivables and submit  the  same  to
the Municipal Accountant for proper accrual of amount  due  to  reflect  the
correct RPT/SET receivable balance at year-end.

Recommendations:

      Ensure that  funds  are  utilized  in  accordance  with  its  intended
purpose and fast track the implementation of the PPAs during the year.

Recommendations:


      Ensure that charges to the fund are aligned with its purpose and  fast
track the implementation of PPAs during the year such  that  the  objectives
for the provision of said funds may be met.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant follow  the  required
amount of employer premium contribution as authorized under R.A. 9679.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the following:

   1. Submit a written justification for the  failure  to  submit  for  post
      audit the DVs and supporting documents on cash  advances  granted  for
      CF.

   2. Strictly adhere to the guidelines on the grant and liquidation of cash
      advances for CF.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the following:

     1.  Ensure  that  cash  advances  are  supported  with  the   required
        documentation;
     2. Observe strictly the proper recording of the grant and  liquidation
        of cash advances.

Recommendations:
      We recommended the following:


   1. Designate another permanently appointed official/employee  to  perform
      disbursing  functions  and  ensure  that  the  disbursing  officer  is
      adequately bonded.
   2. Adhere strictly to the laws, rules and regulations  in  the  granting,
      utilization and liquidation of cash advances as required under Section
      339 of R.A. 7160 and Section 4.1.4 of COA Circular 97-002.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the following courses of action:


     a. Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash  advances
        thru the issuance of letters of demand to officials  and  employees
        concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of  COA  Circular
        97-002  in  the  granting,  utilization  and  liquidation  of  cash
        advances.
     b. Conduct regular and  periodic  verification  and  analysis  of  the
        existence  of  receivables  and  unliquidated  cash   advances   to
        determine extent of collectability.
     c. Request for the  write-off  of  dormant  accounts  and  submit  the
        certified  relevant  documents  validating  the  existence  of  the
        conditions.

Recommendation:

      Direct the Accounting and General Service  Offices  to  reconcile  the
Property, Plant and Equipment accounts.

Recommendation:

      We recommended the LCE  to  direct  the  OIC-Municipal  Accountant  to
compute depreciation expense of the depreciable assets in order to  allocate
its cost over their useful life and for fair presentation  of  the  accounts
in the financial statements.

Recommendation:

      We recommended management to submit Contracts/POs and  its  supporting
documents on time as prescribed  under  Sections  3.1.1  and  3.2.1  of  COA
Circular 2009-001  and  ensure  that  the  same  are  properly  accomplished
indicating such details as date of delivery,  delivery  term,  payment  term
and  date  signed  by  the  supplier.  Priority  should  be  given  to   the
unsubmitted Contracts/POs is highly needed.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that for succeeding fund transfers to be received,  the
concerned personnel should exercise due diligence  in  ensuring  the  timely
and efficient implementation of PPAs.

Recommendation:

      We recommended the allocation of  funds  for  the  completion  of  the
project.

Recommendations:

      We recommended  to  fast-track  the  implementation  of  developmental
projects embodied in the Municipal Annual Investment Plan in  accordance  to
its  time  frame  to  accelerate  socio-economic  growth  and  environmental
development in the municipality.

      We also recommended to the Municipal Planning Development  Council  or
the Municipal Project  Engineer  to  exercise  diligently  their  monitoring
function  such  that  issues  and  concerns  that  cause  delay  in  project
implementation may be immediately identified and addressed.

Recommendation:

      We  recommended  that  management  provide  measures  to  address  the
deficiencies noted in the construction of the  four  (4)  MRFs  to  make  it
operational. Strictly adhere to the provisions of  R.A.  No.  9003  and  its
IRR, particularly on the guidelines in the establishment of MRF.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the following:

   1. Prioritize the creation of the plantilla and hiring of  personnel  for
      MENRO.

   2. Adhere strictly to the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 specifically on the
      duties  and  responsibilities  of  the  MSWMB  to  ensure   the   full
      implementation of the SWM Plan.
We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Development Planning Officer to hasten the implementation of the said above projects.

Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the liquidation of cash advances.  No additional cash advances should be granted unless previous cash advances are already liquidated.
Direct the Local Civil Registrar Officer to cope up with the negative balance of the account by limiting the expenses until such time that collections would be sufficient to cover up for the cash advances made.
observations.managementAction: The negative balance  was already corrected in 2016

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Cash in Bank account per ledger and per cashbook monthly or as often as necessary.
observations.managementAction:
Reconciliation
still in
process

observations.recommendation: Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the aforementioned paid disbursement vouchers to the Commission on Audit so that timely audit of the transactions of the municipality can be performed.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR 2016

observations.recommendation:
Direct the OIC-Accountant to effect necessary adjustments to the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds in the 2 (two) separate books.
Direct the OIC-Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of complete documentation to government transactions such that Memorandum of Agreement and other pertinent documents are attached to the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were already effected in 2016.

observations.recommendation: Submit the contracts and purchase orders including related documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 for its timely review to protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction:
Management has
submitted the
Purchase
Orders only

observations.recommendation: Direct the General Services Office to adjust the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment by removing the Small Value Items. After adjusting the report, require the Accounting Office to also adjust the ledger balances of the Property, Plant and Equipment account.

Direct the Accounting Office to record purchases of supplies and materials as Inventory.
Direct the designated Property and Supply Officer to strictly monitor the issuances of supplies and materials to end users. Requisition and Issue Slip shall be prepared upon issuance of supplies.
The Property and Supply Officer designate shall consolidate the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued and prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and forward to Accounting Unit.
observations.managementAction: Several purchases of supplies and materials were directly charged to expenses
